_doc: Batch 1 — 50 distinct, independent scenarios. Each is a unique transformation. Setup=[shell], verify=[primitive check]. Verify primitives: 'exists/not exists', 'isdir/not isdir', 'size == N', 'md5 <path> == <src_alias>', 'count <dir> == N', 'total_count <dir> == N', 'mode == 0NNN', 'match <shell>'. Aliases: src_pdf_small/large/1..5, src_zip, src_dmg, src_xlsx, src_yaml, src_json.

scenarios.description: Create single empty file, verify it appears
Create directory with 10 empty files inside, verify count
setup: for i in $(seq 1 10); do touch {vfs}/d1/f$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 5 nested directories depth 5, verify each exists
Copy small PDF, verify MD5
Copy large DMG, verify MD5
Copy ZIP archive, verify MD5
Create file, append 1 byte, verify size is original+1
Create file, rename same directory, verify path changed
Create file, rename cross-directory, verify moved
Create file, delete, verify gone
Create empty directory, remove empty directory
Try to remove non-empty directory (rmdir should fail, file remains)
Create 32 empty files (under 64-entry readdir cap), verify count
setup: for i in $(seq 1 32); do touch {vfs}/hundred/f$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 16 directories inside one parent (under cap)
setup: for i in $(seq 1 16); do mkdir {vfs}/parent/d$i; done

scenarios.description: Write then overwrite file, verify final MD5
Truncate file to 50% via python (BSD truncate has no %)
setup: python3 -c 'import os,sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"r+b"); s=os.path.getsize(sys.argv[1]); f.truncate(s//2); f.close()' {vfs}/t

scenarios.description: Copy 5 different files, verify each MD5
Move file up one directory level (cross-parent)
Create deeply nested file at depth 10, read back
setup: python3 -c 'import os,sys; p=sys.argv[1]; parts=["d"+str(i) for i in range(10)]; full=os.path.join(p, *parts); os.makedirs(full, exist_ok=True); open(os.path.join(full, "leaf"), "w").write("deep")' {vfs}

scenarios.description: Rename directory, verify contents moved with it
Copy file with spaces in name
Copy file with unicode in name
Empty filename rejected (touch fails)
Long filename (200 chars), create and verify
Write file, snapshot via .vfsctl, write another file, both visible
Write file, MD5 via python chunked read
Overwrite first half with zeros, size unchanged
setup: python3 -c 'import os,sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"r+b"); s=os.path.getsize(sys.argv[1]); f.write(b"\0"*(s//2)); f.close()' {vfs}/o

scenarios.description: Create 4 dirs, each with 4 files (16 total within readdir cap)
setup: for d in $(seq 1 4); do mkdir {vfs}/d$d; for f in $(seq 1 4); do touch {vfs}/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Move directory tree into another directory
Copy then chmod 0, file still exists
Stat file after copy, verify size
Create 1.5MB file, verify size
Copy ZIP, verify exact size and MD5
Create 32 empty files (under cap), verify count
setup: for i in $(seq 1 32); do touch {vfs}/f$i; done

scenarios.description: Create 32 then delete 16, verify 16 remain
setup: for i in $(seq 1 32); do touch {vfs}/f$i; done
for i in $(seq 1 16); do rm {vfs}/f$i; done

scenarios.description: Recursive copy of directory tree (3 levels, 3 files each)
Write 32 small files sequentially, verify count
setup: for i in $(seq 1 32); do echo $i > {vfs}/w$i; done

scenarios.description: Append across page boundary (8KB + 2KB = 10KB)
Snapshot, modify file, snapshot again
Create directory and a file inside it via single cp
Read file from start, MD5 check
Create file in non-existent dir should fail
Copy all Downloads PDFs into VFS (mixed sizes, partial)
setup: for f in /Users/bogdanadnan/Downloads/*.pdf; do cp "$f" {vfs}/ 2>/dev/null || true; done

scenarios.description: Read last 100 bytes of a file via tail
Create 8 dirs, each with 4 files (32 total within cap)
setup: for d in $(seq 1 8); do mkdir {vfs}/q$d; for f in $(seq 1 4); do touch {vfs}/q$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create file with mode 0644, verify mode
Remove all 20 files via glob, verify empty
setup: for i in $(seq 1 20); do touch {vfs}/x$i; done

scenarios.description: Chained rename a→b→c, verify c exists, a and b don't
Create 5 sibling files with similar names
Write file, verify it survives unmount/remount
